title: sapindaceae
intro: The Sapindaceae rank, also known as the soapberry or lychee family, refers to a group of flowering plants consisting of about 130 genera and 1,300 species. This family is primarily composed of trees, shrubs, and vines, often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Sapindaceae includes a diverse range of plants, such as raintrees, lychees, and soapberries, which are known for their edible fruits and seeds. Many species within this family are cultivated for their fruit, timber, and ornamental value, while others have been used in traditional medicine and as food sources.
